reviews.review_msg: Matthew is an amazing prof. The homework (7 assignments in total) isn't too hard, but detailed enough that it'll give you a good understanding of the respective topic. For midterms and final, going through the homework is enough, but there's practice midterms too.

(152) matthew was a great prof, he barely got through all the slides but i think it was just because the first few units had so much to cover. but he really did his best on helping us understand. exams were fair nothing that was outside of what he taught us in class

(MATH 152) Took me a bit to get the hype, but Matt taught the course well. More importantly to me though, I thought his tests were extremely fair, and there wasn't anything to catch you off guard. Very manageable. Dude doesn't respond to emails though, so if you need to get a hold of him you'll have to catch him in person.
Amazing professor, one of the best I've ever had.

Amazing lecturer, passionate, but definitely has some problematic ways of running the course. 

Rather than releasing weekly textbook questions along with the weekly WebAssigns, he releases all those weeks worth of textbook questions a week before every exam. 

Never once has replied to an email of mine.

Other than that, he's a great CALC2 prof.

DeVos is an amazing professor, so kind and enthusiastic, has a compassionate approach for an upper level math course. Assignments were good practice and questions were taken from the textbook. Exams were based on assignments and lectures, and were very fair. I wish he could teach all of my math courses.

DeVos is simply awesome. Explains concepts intuitively, very approachable and enthusiastic, fair assignments and exams. It was great taking Advanced Linear Algebra with him this semester. It was his first time teaching this course and hopefully not his last.
Amazing. Took 2 math 301 and 304 with him. Fair assignments and exams. Give practise questions and exams are very similar. He is the best math prof i have had at SFU.

Matt is a fantastic prof. He is very willing to help you succeed in his course! Weekly homework gives you a good review of the material. Sample exam and practice questions can greatly help you prepare for exams. He also provides the free textbook for students! Just read through the book and ask questions. Learn Geometry and Symmetry with him!

Very caring upbeat prof. Makes sure every student understands and goes into great detail to answer questions. Only problem is his teaching style is a bit disorganized and i didn't know why were learning certain things. The homework was much harder than the exams. Exam were very easy and fair. Similar to the samples he gives

Such a jolly person. My only criticism is that he stutters like crazy in lecture and can often take a while to get to the point, thus why I'm giving 4. The assignments helped teach us almost everything on the exams. Final project was very free in regards to what we wanted to do it on. Super accessible for questions after class. Stress-free course.

Matthew is definitely one of the best math profs at SFU. His passion in his lectures is great and it really keeps you engaged. The assessments were very straight forward and reasonable. This class is definitely very tough but with him, it was a breeze. He's super approachable and his teaching style is awesome, would recommend 10000%.

Matthew is very passionate about MACM and it shows, he also cares about his students. I did find his lectures slightly scattered (would jump between points while explaining) or rushed sometimes, which didn't match my learning style great (obviously might be different for you). BUT he is very engaging and the quizzes and exams were fair. Great prof!

MATH 304 - Geometry and Symmetry

Gives clear lectures and encourages questions after class if you have difficulty with concepts or (especially) homework. His exams were fair and allowed me to demonstrate my knowledge of the material. If you see this course being offered by him, I recommend taking it because it was generally easy to perform well.
He knows the content very well, but has difficulty explaining it. Very kind and considerate, but not great at teaching. Save yourself some time and watch Jamie Mulholland's video series instead.

Dr. Devos is a great proff! He is very caring and takes the time to actually listen to your concerns. Just talk to the guy and you will feel more confident in the subject. His lectures are quick and the pace of the class is quite demanding but it is to be expected from a MACM class. Makes MACM a bit let threatening so he deserves a 5/5.

He's a very interesting/passionate guy, but his lectures weren't helpful and I preferred to just study at home.

He doesn't upload assignment solutions online, which is a pretty big downside.

Assignments and tests are difficult, but normally they're either curved or only evaluated based on the easier questions. I didn't find this class tough.

Don't take this course if you don't need to. Not for human. All course materials are not making sense. If the course wasn't taken with DeVos, must have whole class failed. He will curve! He is a great prof. Hope he teaches all upper level math.

Not bad. He is always nervous. Final were similar to the practice final. Study practice final to prepare. Try talk to him if you are not doing good in class, he could help you by looking through your progress. Seldom reply email, find him in person.
Was open to answering questions during class, made note taking engaging, and was thorough in explaining calculus concepts.

Knows well and explains well, although can be absent-minded sometimes. Homework every week to be tested in quizzes, the usual LD math setup. Exams are fair and reasonable in length. Does not expect you to have crazy deep knowledge of theorems at this level. I recommend him for any math class.

Devos is a great prof. He takes time to explain concepts carefully in a way that's understandable. You can tell he's passionate about what he teaches. As long as you attend lectures and follow along and review after class, this course should be okay!

DeVos was a pretty standard teacher in terms of lectures. You show up to class, listen, go home and do your homework assignments. The content of the course is not difficult, IF you study and keep on top of your homework. Personally, I thought DeVos did a great job of explaining material, and he posts lecture notes after class. I highly recommend!

DeVos is very passionate about what he teaches and explains the concepts very clearly. Although I still reviewed after class, he made the material much more clear. There was some delay in answering emails and uploading notes, but it was not too much of an issue. Overall solid prof, I wouldn't mind taking another math class with him.
Really good lectures. Hes pretty slow to upload things and respond to emails, but I really enjoyed how he taught the class.

Matt is a good prof, explains the concept clearly. He releases a big review package that resembles the actual exam before midterms and finals, which is really helpful. The only con is he gets distracted easily during lectures and often can't finish the material in time.

I like him but I hate his lecture. One of the worst prof at explaining things, especially for such an abstract subject. The course material itself is kinda hard already and his lecture definitely makes life harder... But he always scales the grade based on overall performance of you and the class, so if you work hard you can still get decent grade.

Tends to make his tests too difficult/long. Before a midterm, he said that it would be straightforward, but then afterward he said that on second thought it was too technical. Class average was a C+. His lectures were clear enough and he keeps his word (kept a quiz cancelled even when a transit strike gets cancelled, and he curves the tests).

This guy's the best math professor I've ever had. He made such an intense, theory heavy course look doable. Clears doubts in a class of 450! Really good at explaining concepts. Gave a 5% curve on both midterms. The quizzes were 9/10 at least, if you do the homework problems. This guy is real passionate for the subject.

Well-organized notes. Great oral speaker. Occasionally rushes through some examples because of time constraints but his office hour explanations were absolutely fantastic. I learned a lot from him that I otherwise would not have with another prof. Superb knowledge and communicative skills. He's also very understanding when it comes to exams.

Made a notoriously hard course easier than I expected. Exam questions were just slight variations of the quiz / homework questions for the most part. He skipped over some of the less important / hard parts which was nice (2nd part of GFs, probability, etc). Kinda frazzled during lectures but still explains stuff clearly.

Probably the best prof for MACM 201. Matt did a good job making a hard course a little easier. Some lectures were poorly paced and he would lose track of time but overall they were good. Did extremely helpful review sessions before the midterms and final. Curved the midterm scores (i.e for the first midterm 22/30 was an A)

Rarely prepared for lectures. Notes are not uploaded in a timely manner. If you find him outside of class time, he will stay hours to explain any topics you want. Midterms are what is covered in lectures and quizzes. Only the final exam required thinking outside the box.

This professor is simply a beauty. Has decent explanations of concepts and makes a hard course more straightforward. Quiz and exam questions are just a variation of the non-graded homework. He sometimes was slow to upload notes to Canvas; one note package was uploaded the morning of the final. However, the workshop can clarify concepts very well.
No curve in the class. Lecture is very slow and he sometime forgot to upload the note. The last lecture note was upload on the morning of the final day. Not suggesting him.

Awesome lecturer for MACM 201. Perfectly explained what was going to be on each midterm and final. Went a little slow in some lectures and forced us to learn material on our own sometimes (due to him running out of time). Otherwise awesome guy with a great teaching style.

He explains concepts very well, although he re-explains the same concepts multiple times before he moves on so he often runs out of time. Exams are as fair as they can get. Devos makes a GPA killer course into a GPA booster.

Fantastic prof for MACM201. He knows theres A LOT of material to go over and doesnt try to make trick questions to stump you. He puts fair questions that are based on the assignments and it shows in how well the class does. Thank you Matt!

Good lectures, but slow, the guy's a bit nervous. Very fair exams, similar conceptually to homework, as well as in terms of difficulty. What you're expected to know and do is very clearly outlined. Cares alot about student concerns. Took a notoriously difficult course and made it easier, which to some can be taken as a bad thing. Not for me though.
Good lecturer, makes a boring course bearable. Lots of energy and explains concepts well.
He is a good prof and his exams are easier than usual. He gives a limited amount of maths for practice and all the exam question comes in that pattern. HE IS NOT ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS.

Leverages lecture and homework material for exams, sometimes even the same questions with slight additions and twists. Is very knowledgeable about the course, but tends to proceed at a snails pace in some lectures. Is not the best at keeping up with posting course material with any sort of punctuality but still gets the job done eventually.

He is an very good prof, Exams and quizzes are fair. As long as you show up for every class and make sure you study the assignments you will do well on the quizzes and exam. I've heard a lot of people saying MACM201 is going to hard but he is very good at explaining the concepts

First time I've left a review - Dr. DeVos knows his stuff well. While he occasionally stumbles with his words in lecture it's only because the material is so deep and so interesting for him that he can't help it, plus the fact that the class sizes are huge. You definitely need to read the textbook and do all homework problems to get a good grade.
Very passionate professor and makes a harsh course very very interesting and easier gets his questions from the quizzes, take it with him if you can

Great professor. Very accessible and will take the extra time to explain anything you may not understand. Lessons follow an easy to understand progression and lecture notes are clear and easily understandable

Devos might be an anxious guy but his lecture examples are very clear and you will see them on tests. Tests were very much like the practice exams he provides. He might not have the best teaching skills but his easy exams make up for it. You will probably have to teach yourself most of the material but it is doable. Class average of B-.

A real gem in the math department. His lectures are a bit all over the place, but his tests are so similar to the homework so it makes up for any confusion. Most importantly, he is approachable with any difficult concepts or proofs and will work through them with you till you understand. Take number theory or geometry with him, you won't regret it!
he rambles and really hard to comprehend what he's trying to explain. Work hard you'll do good. But I honestly cant listen to the guy.

He's a bit frazzled during lectures lol. OQ isn't very related to lectures and some IQs are ?? but everything is doable. Go to the workshop for help with hard IQ questions. Everyone there is really helpful. If you understand calc/took it in high school or smth, you should be fine. I found stuff after midterm1 harder but it was an ok class overall
Down to earth prof, very passionate. If you pay attention in lecture and do the review he gives before mid terms and finals, your golden.

Devos is an amazing prof. He's super caring and really explains to the best of his abilities. If you pay attention in class, you're golden. After lecture, you can ask him to explain something and he'll explain till you get it. Midterms and finals are very fair with all the questions coming straight from the slides. Really wish I have him again.

Matt tends to spend an equal amount of time on each chapter which sometimes sucks as certain chapters have more difficult concepts to grasp than others. He's very approachable, but prefers people not to ask questions during lecture as he falls behind quite a bit (explaining trivia questions). His exams are fair/based on examples in notes/IQs

Seems extremely anxious during class, causing him to stutter and be difficult to understand. Skims over content quickly, if you're not paying attention you will fall behind and have no idea whats going on. Outside of class he seems to be a caring professor, the only issue is that he has difficulty communicating effectively.

I think he is one of the best math profs at SFU (no accent & explains concepts clearly). Calc I is easy. If his notes don't make sense, read the textbook for further info and hints for those IQ's. They're not supposed to be easy. Go to the workshop if you need help. It's there for a reason. Calc II is waywayway harder btw.

I have been in this class for 3 weeks and have genuinely no idea what's going on. I put this class off and took it after reading all these decent reviews for Matt. He's constantly disorganized and doesn't seem to know what's going on. The MathXL online questions barely relate to the material and his IQs are incredibly hard and unrelated to lecture.

He rambles about formulas and isn't clear during lectures. If you get math then math 154 is probably going to be alright regardless of what prof you have. His instructor questions are REALLY hard. I love math but the IQ and midterms brought my mark down.

One of the best profs I've ever taken a class with. True that sometimes seemed a little rushed, but ultimately got his material across and he was not responsible for mapleTA malfunctions. Provides great 1 on 1 feedback in workshop. Fairest exams I've ever taken, very similar to notes and assignments. Very understanding and makes lectures engaging.

Despite the hate Matthew got, I thought he was an excellent professor. I always feared math and had been putting this class off for a long time, and I was very happy that I got Matthew as a professor. Some aspects of the course were messy, but Devos made up for it. Exams were very fair.
Really good at explaining tough concepts. He has excellent notes, but sometimes does proofs differently in class, so just be aware. If you have a chance to take a graph theory class with him, do it!

Extremely unorganized during lectures- something went wrong at least once each class. Seemed frazzled much of the time. Very nice guy outside of class and is a decent 1 on 1 teacher, but he doesn't get his point across well in lecture. Often made many mistakes which he had to correct later on. Learned more off of additional sources than his lecture

this course was a gong show but it wasn't devos's fault. He was very willing to fix problems and accomadate when others involved in the course didn't do their jobs. While his lectures weren't the clearest, but he is great to talk to 1 on 1. He is super patient and thorough during office hours and the midterms were good. his flickr is A+

He is an excellent professor who clearly puts time and effort into teaching his class. He  makes sure you understand the material before moving onto other topics, and unlike most other professors, asks for the students' feedback on their learning experience.

The course itself is not very hard, however, i learned most of the material either by myself or from the internet as his lecture notes were very unclear and had consistent mistakes. He often wasted time in lecture with irrelevant i-clicker questions, and the only online practice we had was not consistent and terrible to use.

Matts speaks clearly and explains concepts well and will keep trying with you until you understand. His tests were very fair and reflective of the materiel. Maple TA was an absolute disaster for most people but in his defence he wasn't responsible for it. His review sheets were also very helpful. I wish he was available more often in the workshop

Honestly for a calc course I feel like he's one of the best you can get. He definitely has his off days; however, his exams were fair and lined up almost perfectly with his notes and review sheets.  MapleTA and the textbook are trash so those two are what made the class the most annoying, both of  which Matt didn't really have anything to do with.

I really enjoyed my experience with Matt and with SFU Math in general. The workshop set up is far superior to having a tutorial. Matt's exams were really fair. Reading the textbook is unnecessary. If you are able to understand the concepts taught in class, you are golden. Just make sure you're great with the quadratic formula (oops).

His midterms and final exam are based on the lectures and what he has actually taught! So getting a good grade is fairly easy, study the notes, and examples and you'll be golden! The marking is fair and the questions are fiar too
He means well :)

Gives little to no relevant practice material, and when asked by class to give more, he removed our online homework program and did nothing else. This professor loves math, but teaches through his own mindset which is unfortunately incompatible with the average student. Makes a ton of algebra mistakes in his lecture notes and shows minimal work.

His lectures aren't very clear in helping you understand. He makes a lot of mistakes in class, which is quite confusing but also hilarious. He has a lot of instructor questions that are just trivia but worth for participation marks. He always seems very rushed getting to class on time and finishing the lecture in time.
He is very passionate, especially when you talk to him in person. Also, when you go to his office hours, he explains an problem that you may have clearly and understandingly.

Very enthusiastic teacher, you can tell he really enjoys teaching Math. He likes to bring up real-life concepts to show that what we learn in Calculus is actually very closely connected to the real world.

You need to practice a lot on your own!! He explain things really well and makes calc easy. He seems to have bad days but still manages to give great lectures. Make use of the calc workshop and get lots of practice.
Very good class, easy exams and no surprises. Show up for lectures, study and you will do well. Very good professor

Professor Devos is oftentimes disorganized and seems to have off days, however, overall, he seems to have a great passion for mathematics and likes to point out applications in the real world to make it more interesting. He teaches a tough course, but I feel like he, himself is a great teacher despite his quirks.

Honestly, this is my first time giving a rating to any professor and probably my last, but this professor truly deserves such great ratings. I have taken this course several semesters ago and failed miserably. Fortunately, this time around I came across such a  passionate prof who would explain concept in ways that normal people could understand.

Hard course but very nice prof. He's obviously passionate about this course. I could not attend his lecture because of the conflict of another mandatory course that was not offered frequently. Assignments are difficult. I got tons of help from his office hours. I could not have survived this course otherwise.
helpful
the best professor in SFU. The exams are not tricky, the number of assignments questions are less than every other math professors. He's so nice,wish can take his courses later.

If you can take a class with Matt, take it, no matter what he's teaching. Very clear and well written notes. Assignments and exams are a bit tricky but they are marked very fairly. He's an entertaining lecturer and get's really excited about math related things.
The best professor in SFU.
Decent professor if you talk to him, but tutorials and lectures were uncoordinated. Homework and test material were not even close to the same type of questions. Average on final was 43%.
He liked talking about real life applications of math. Midterms and final were fair. He was a clear lecturer, but seemed disorganized. He is a good prof.
DeVos is the man! so much passion for the maths, great prof.

Wow, I've never seen such enthusiam from anyone! This man has a passion for mathematics and it shows. He makes the hardest concepts really simple. Midterm, were ok. But the final, aside from the proofs, was generally easy. Would definitely recommend taking any courses, if possible, with him!!
He is a great prof. Definitely take this class with him if you can.

Fun class for sure, definitely and interesting teacher. By far my favorite class this semester. Homework and Exams were fair and relatively easy. I definitely recommend taking math 304 (loved the games with Rose and Colin)

very hard on the final exam; lectures based on his ppt notes. Just bring them to class and do tons of practices will save you in the midterm and final. Final is very hard, not from the textbook, so watch out.

Great teacher. Matt is super cool and easy going. He explains everything very clearlly. He makes calculas easy and somewhat interesting. I highly recommend taking Calculas with Matt. As well his exams are fairly easy.

He`s a nice guy but he acts as if he`s teaching calculus grade 8s. Goes over material very slowly and never finishes on time. Spends too long repeating himself instead of doing examples that might help us learn. Exams are quite easy though (1st midterm average was 80%).
Great prof. He's funny, interesting, and easy. If you want a good mark in calculus, DEFINITELY take it with DeVos. :)

He's a really good prof, very helpful. He explain everything so clearly, if you are not a math person, he's a good prof to take. He doesn't act like you're stupid if you don't understand something. He tries to help out as much as possible.

Great class! He explains the concepts really well, and doesn't express frustration with redundant questions in lectures. The assignments were always harder than his exams, so everyone did really well! He was in the Math lab every week, and the tests were always more than fair. The average was decent for a first-year class.

Great prof! He goes through everything step by step and makes the material very easy to understand. He puts solutions online and if you do all the homework and practice problems before exams you will do great!

Great Prof! He is very clear and explains everything so coherently. He doesn't have a lot in the way of office hours but way they are provided he goes above and beyond to make sure you understand. Tests are very fair mid-term 1 avg: 70%, mid-term 2 avg:69%, final avg: 60.5%.
if you want to get a REALLY good mark for calculus.. take his class. class average for his tests are usually in the 65-70%. great prof!
He actually explains and I'm able to understand what he's talking about. I use to LOVE math until I took it with another proff and failed it. But Devos is really good at his explanations.
very fun, very nice, made me really enjoy math

fun and interesting, and very helpful, I missed a couple of classes and went to ask a question about the material, and he sum up the lectues making sure I had a handle on all the material. He's also very helpful with homework, which is good because some of it is auite challenging

Simply one of the best math profs. The midterms were very easy and very clear. Just do the HW problems. I don't know about the final. We'll have to wait and see. He makes calculus very easy. Great job.

Very Clear and concise lectures, doesn't bore you with useless notes. He really knows how to get across his points to the class. Good at keeping you interested in the material b/c he gives lots of applications. He makes funny drawings. GREAT PROF!!

Yeah! It's so funny when he plots graphs with his arms. His examples are very down to earth. He is good at explaining why things are the way they are, and he really makes you understand what he is saying. He makes getting up at 6:30 to make it to class worth the effort. Very glad I got to take this class with him.

his examples are clear, makes few mistakes, funny guy he plots graps on overhead and with his body =D , tho inclass he gives few examples, yet their very descriptive. good guy , seems very excited about teaching

Good prof. He seems a bit unsure about how to explain some things; I think he's new to teaching. Really funny though. One day he wanted to emphasize something, so he started jumping up and down to get our attention. Still waiting for the first midterm to see how hard it will be, but he seems very friendly and fair.
No Comments
